pi_items.source: verifier.py checks for DASHBOARD_NAME == 'Weekly Ops Review'

metadata_value: CPU This Week vs Last Week
verified_value: CPU This Week vs Last Week
pi_items.source: verifier.py checks for G1_TITLE, G2_TITLE, and G3_TITLE exactly matching these strings
feed_real_data.py maps NAB CSV files to these exact Graphite metric paths. verifier.py also checks for substrings of these paths.
task.json description sets the persona to 'Network Operations Center (NOC) analyst'
No evidence found in scripts or NAB dataset documentation that these specific EC2 instances belong to an E-commerce company.
export_result.sh packages and saves the output to /tmp/weekly_ops_comparison_result.json and verifier.py reads from this path

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on real server metrics from the Numenta Anomaly Benchmark (NAB) dataset, which are injected into Graphite by feed_real_data.py. The expected dashboard name 'Weekly Ops Review' and the three specific graph titles are strictly checked by verifier.py. The required metric paths (e.g. 'servers.ec2_instance_1.cpu.utilization') exactly match the metric structure generated from the real NAB CSV files. The 'E-commerce' industry claim is unsupported by the underlying NAB data or scripts.
